sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Mallorca have shown mixed form in their last 5 matches with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, highlighted by a strong 3-1 away victory against Sevilla. Levante UD, on the other hand, have struggled recently, managing just 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses in their last 5, with a heavy 0-3 home defeat to Rayo Vallecano. Mallorca seem to have a slight edge in confidence going into this match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last 2 head-to-head encounters, each team has secured a win, with Levante UD triumphing 2-0 in their most recent meeting in January 2022, and Mallorca winning 1-0 at home in October 2021. Historically, matches between these sides tend to be tight, often with low-scoring outcomes and fewer corners.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Both teams are closely matched in the league table, with Mallorca at 17th and Levante UD at 16th, each on 8 points. Mallorca have scored 10 goals but struggle with possession (43.4%, 16th in the league), while Levante UD have netted 13 but conceded 17, showing defensive vulnerabilities. Mallorca's efficiency in converting big chances (58.3%, 1st in the league) could be a deciding factor.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Mallorca's goalkeeper Leo Román has been in fine form with an average Sofascore rating of 7.66 over the last 5 matches, potentially crucial in keeping Levante at bay. For Levante UD, forward Etta Eyong has been a standout with 3 goals and 1 assist in recent games, posing a significant threat to Mallorca's defense.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given the Sofascore probabilities (39% home win, 23% draw, 38% away win) and current form, this match looks evenly poised. Mallorca's home advantage at Estadi de Son Moix and recent attacking output slightly tilt the scales in their favor, but Levante's ability to score in bursts cannot be ignored. Expect a low-scoring, competitive game with a likelihood of late goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Levante UD leads Mallorca 1-0 in this matchup at Estadi de Son Moix. The away side capitalized on a clinical moment early in the game to take the lead, while Mallorca, despite dominating possession and creating several opportunities, has been unable to convert their chances into goals. The home team will be looking to turn their control into tangible results in the second half, while Levante UD will aim to defend their advantage and exploit any counter-attacking opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Mallorca's dominance and missed opportunities
halftimeSections.text: Mallorca has been the dominant force in terms of possession and territory throughout the first half. They have created numerous big chances but have been wasteful in front of goal, missing key opportunities to level the score. Their frequent entries into the final third and penalty area show a clear intent to attack, but the finishing touch has been lacking. Players like Vedat Muriqi and Mateo Joseph have been heavily involved in the attack, with the latter showing significant threat, yet they couldn’t capitalize on their efforts. Mallorca’s high pressing and ability to win duels in the final third suggest they can turn the game around if they improve their finishing.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Levante UD's defensive resilience and clinical edge
halftimeSections.text: Levante UD, despite having less of the ball, have shown a pragmatic approach with a solid defensive structure. They have been efficient with their limited attacking forays, taking the lead through Etta Eyong’s well-taken goal. Their ability to withstand Mallorca’s pressure while maintaining a threat on the break has been key to their first-half success. The away side has managed to limit Mallorca’s clear-cut chances with disciplined defending, and their goalkeeper has played a crucial role in keeping the scoreline in their favor. Levante UD will likely focus on maintaining this compact shape in the second half to protect their lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Mallorca consistently pushing forward with sustained attacking spells, especially in the middle and latter stages of the half. They dictated the tempo for long periods, with multiple waves of pressure on Levante UD’s defense. However, their momentum was briefly interrupted by a shift in focus when Levante UD mounted a decisive attacking move to score their goal. After conceding, Mallorca regained control and continued to press relentlessly, showing a clear intent to equalize. Levante UD, on the other hand, had fewer attacking moments but made the most of their key spell to take the lead, demonstrating efficiency over volume.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Mallorca, Mateo Joseph stands out as a potential game-changer in the second half due to his involvement in creating and nearly finishing big chances. His ability to find dangerous positions could be crucial if Mallorca are to get back into the game. Sergi Darder and Manu Morlanes have also been pivotal in orchestrating attacks, with their key passes setting up opportunities. On Levante UD’s side, Etta Eyong remains a threat after scoring the opener, and his ability to exploit spaces on the counter could hurt Mallorca again. Additionally, goalkeeper Mathew Ryan’s strong performance has been vital in maintaining Levante UD’s lead and will be critical in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Mallorca intensify their attacking efforts as they chase an equalizer. Given their possession dominance and the number of chances created, it seems probable they will find the back of the net if they can sharpen their finishing. Levante UD, meanwhile, will likely adopt a more defensive posture, looking to soak up pressure and strike on the counter. Mallorca’s urgency could leave gaps at the back, which Levante UD might exploit. Tactical adjustments, such as potential substitutions to inject fresh legs or creativity for Mallorca, could play a significant role in breaking the deadlock.
